instructions: Edit use to 'both', 'glossary', or 'ui' and edit definition as needed, then re-run this script.
editing_notes: Terms using both surfaces appear first, then glossary-only, then ui-only.
Group is the display section, preferring Finance section when present.
Use controls whether the definition should exist in both docs, only Finance, or only the UI docs page.

terms.definition: Historical closing price adjusted for corporate actions (splits, dividends) to maintain a consistent series.
Backtesting methodology where the model is trained on a rolling window and tested on the next unseen period, reducing overfitting.
Rate of change in an option's price relative to a $1 move in the underlying asset. Ranges 0–1 for calls, 0 to -1 for puts.
Number of calendar days until an options contract expires.
Normalized 0–100 score for current implied volatility vs. its 1-year range. 0 = near lowest; 100 = near highest. Used to gauge whether options are relatively cheap or expensive.
Long-term Equity Anticipation Securities — options with expirations typically one year or more out.
The price paid (or received) for an options contract.
A predefined exit point to limit losses on a trade.
The price at which an option contract can be exercised.
A predefined exit point to lock in gains on a trade.
Excess return of a strategy relative to a benchmark. Positive alpha means outperformance.
A reference index (e.g., SPY, QQQ) used to compare strategy performance.
A point-in-time record of total portfolio value used to build a performance time series.
Total realized and unrealized gain or loss on a position or portfolio.
Gain or loss locked in by closing a position.
Difference between the expected execution price and the actual fill price.
Gain or loss on a position still open (mark-to-market).
Exponential Moving Average — applies more weight to recent prices than older ones.
Moving Average Convergence Divergence — momentum indicator comparing two EMAs and a signal line.
Bet that prices will revert to a historical average after an extreme move.
The persistence of price trends — assets that have performed well recently tend to continue doing so over short horizons.
Smoothed price series over a rolling window (e.g., 50-day MA). Used to identify trend direction.
Relative Strength Index (0–100) measuring speed and magnitude of recent price changes. Above 70 = overbought; below 30 = oversold.
Number of shares or contracts traded in a period — a key confirmation signal for price moves.
Error where future data leaks into historical signal generation.
Persistent market state (for example trending, mean-reverting, high-volatility).
Distortion from only including assets that still exist today.
Sensitivity of an asset's returns to market movements. Beta of 1.0 moves in line with the market.
Percentage of trades that are profitable.
Largest peak-to-trough decline in portfolio value.
Risk-adjusted return: mean excess return divided by standard deviation. Higher is better.
How frequently positions are replaced; high turnover can increase transaction costs.
Average True Range, a volatility measure often used for position sizing and stops.
Volatility bands around a moving average.
Price move beyond support or resistance, often on elevated volume.
Digital assets via feeds like ccxt — not currently active.
Individual stocks — core focus area.
Sector, factor, and index ETFs — useful for regime and trend strategies.
Currency pairs — not currently active.
Commodity and index futures — not currently active.
Macro-level signals (rates, volatility indices) for regime context.
Long-dated options used to simulate leveraged equity exposure with defined risk.
Use quantitative factors (value, quality, momentum, low-volatility) to rank and select assets.
Rank assets by recent relative performance and go long the top performers.
Exploit temporary divergences in the price spread between historically correlated instruments.
Adjust strategy behavior based on detected market regime (trending vs. mean-reverting, etc.).
Enter positions in the direction of an established price trend; exit when the trend weakens.
Enter positions when price breaks out of a defined volatility range.
